data.body:
1Overview

This epic tracks everything needed to make Small Hours work seamlessly with an AI bot player (Claude), and to polish the existing games based on first-hand playtesting feedback.

Playtested on 2026-04-12 with Claude joining as a player via the browser. Key findings drove the issues below.

---

## Areas

### 🤖 Bot Integration

* Add a WebSocket bot client interface (structured game state + action API)
* Expose a `BOT_ACTION` message type so bots can submit moves without a browser UI
* Add bot player registration to lobby (join with `?bot=true` flag or dedicated endpoint)
* Add a game state event stream endpoint for bot consumption
* Document the game engine action/state contract in README

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

* Gin Rummy: Draw action has no clickable target — game gets completely stuck
* Lobby chat: Player names missing from messages (shows `: message` instead of `name: message`)

### ✨ Game Polish

* Gin Rummy: Fix draw mechanic — stock pile and discard pile need to be clickable
* Quiz: Add a visible countdown timer on the player screen
* Shithead: Show opponent face-up cards so players can strategise

---

## Motivation

Claude joined as a player via the browser and played Quiz, Gin Rummy and Shithead. Quiz and Shithead UIs felt solid and intuitive. Gin Rummy was completely broken due to missing draw interaction. For the longer term vision of having Claude as a proper bot player (rather than clicking a browser), a lightweight structured interface would make this much more reliable and fast.

issue.title: Review AI Bot Player integration and game polish issues (converted to project)
